content.text: The PO-33 stores 16 independent patterns, each containing its own sequence of notes and automation. Patterns can be selected on the fly for live performance or chained together for longer song arrangements.
Turn the pattern knob to select patterns 1-16. The current pattern number is shown on the display. Pattern changes can be made while stopped or during playback.

content.title: Live Pattern Changes
content.text: When changing patterns during playback, the new pattern starts at the beginning of the next bar for seamless transitions.
You can copy the contents of one pattern to another for creating variations.

items: Select the source pattern you want to copy
Turn pattern knob to the destination pattern number
Release record - the pattern is now copied

content.text: Pattern chain mode lets you arrange multiple patterns into a sequence that plays automatically. This is useful for creating longer song structures without manual pattern changes.

items: While holding play, turn pattern knob to select start pattern
Continue holding play, turn pattern knob to select end pattern
Release play - chain is now set
Press play to start - patterns play in sequence and loop

content.title: Chain Range
content.text: Pattern chains always play consecutive patterns from start to end. For example, chaining patterns 1-4 plays: 1, 2, 3, 4, 1, 2, 3, 4...
To clear all notes from a pattern and start fresh:

items: Select the pattern you want to clear
All notes are removed from the pattern

content.title: No Undo
content.text: Clearing a pattern cannot be undone. Make sure you've selected the correct pattern before clearing.

items: Use pattern 16 as a 'scratch pad' for experimenting before copying to other slots
Create variations by copying a pattern and making small changes
Chain patterns in performance order for complete song arrangements
Keep some patterns empty as breaks or transitions
